mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the trigger activates, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during inflationary periods or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.4 percent trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >65% of high-inflation months or the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters with positive GDP growth or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.04 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.3 percent signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate output, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in >55% of triggered instances or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.
